text:
BOTTOM LINE — Broad risk-on, small caps and semis quietly took control. Indices rose with IWM +0.93% and RSP +0.91% outpacing SPY +0.39% as breadth and small-cap value volume (IJS 10.9x) signaled rotation beyond mega-cap leaders. Tech strength (XLK +1.00% and Semi 7 +3.04%) tied to AI optimism in Nvidia, Tesla, Qualcomm, Micron, Intel and SOXX +2.41% combined with easing geopolitical risk and benign liquidity (stress 37/100) to fuel a higher-equity, lower-gold (GLD -0.76%) tape. Options skew (SPY PCR 1.26 and QQQ PCR 1.57 with positive skew) showed ongoing protection demand even as Fear & Greed at 57.9 stayed neutral, suggesting a climbing-the-wall-of-worry grind rather than euphoria.

INDICES — VIX closed at 16.7, down vs the 20-day MA 17.6. Returns ranked: IWM +0.93%, QQQ +0.42%, SPY +0.39%, DIA +0.60% so order was IWM +0.93%, DIA +0.60%, QQQ +0.42%, SPY +0.39%. STYLE showed Small > large by 0.51pp with small +0.93% vs large +0.42%, while the key cross-asset move was USO -1.14% against TLT +0.55%. SPY above its 200-day MA saw no CTA sell trigger while QQQ +0.42% also remained above its long-term trend.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran around 0.9x average while QQQ printed 0.8x, pointing to moderate participation in large-cap beta. Volume breadth showed DIA 1.1x and RSP 1.1x gaining attention along with XLE 1.1x and XLK 0.9x, while XLP 0.7x, XLB 0.7x, and XLY 0.7x were ignored. The combination of rising prices and slightly elevated volume in cyclicals (XLE 1.1x with price +0.61%) signaled quiet accumulation rather than a low-conviction squeeze.

SECTORS — XLV Healthcare +1.17%, XLK Technology +1.00%, XLU Utilities +0.78%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLE Energy +0.61%, XLB Materials +0.54%, XLF Financials +0.41%, XLY Discretionary +0.40%, XLP Staples +0.17%, XLRE Real Estate +0.13%, XLC Comms -0.55%. Top industries were CIBR +2.59% and ROBO +2.42%, while bottom groups were BOAT -2.55% and DRAM -2.80%.

THE 7s — Top baskets were Semi +3.04%, Software +2.41%, Health +1.99%, Buildout +1.95%, Power +1.50%. Bottom baskets were Insurance -0.42%, Housing -0.35%, Old Metals -0.21%, Mag -0.05%, Medtech +0.12%. Semi strength was led by Nvidia and Qualcomm while laggards included weaker memory names in DRAM -2.80%, and in the Housing basket homebuilders dragged despite select building-products resilience.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17 with CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, and 0% hike alongside a stance tally of 6 hawks, 5 neutrals, and 4 doves. USD liquidity stress at 37/100 (Normal) showed Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile as the most-stressed component, with TED spread just 0.09% and ON-RRP near $1.0B. Stable macro prints (CPI YoY 3.95% and unemployment 4.30%) kept policy expectations anchored around the current 3.50%–3.75% Fed funds target.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.26 and QQQ PCR 1.57 with positive skew (+4.0% and +6.2%) highlighted meaningful demand for downside protection even on a green day. Spot remained above GEX flips (SPY 750.0, QQQ 625.0, IWM 285.0), so dealer gamma stayed long and dampened intraday volatility. VIX 16.7 and VXN 22.8 sitting below their 20-day MAs (17.6 and 23.5) contrasted with MOVE 78.4 above its 20-day 74.6, signaling bond-vol stress still elevated versus equity vol.

CROSS-ASSET — Gold and silver weakness (GLD -0.76%, SLV -1.57%) alongside TLT +0.55% and UUP +0.14% reflected a mild duration bid and firmer dollar pressuring metals. Oil’s -1.14% drop in USO even as XLE gained +0.61% showed equity energy names trading more on ceasefire optimism and risk appetite than on front-month crude.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 57.9 labeled sentiment neutral and steady. Biggest movers included INTU +4.19% in Software 7 on 3.4x volume, IJS +0.87% on 10.9x volume in small-cap value, and QCOM around +12% on AI-auto headlines. 20d momentum leaders were XLK +12.59% and Semi +24.57%, while laggards were XLB -3.14% and Medtech -5.24%. Vol spikes in IJS (10.9x with price up) and INTU (3.4x with price up) pointed to accumulation flows into small-cap value and quality software tied to AI narratives.

WATCH — IJS +0.87% deserves attention with 10.9x volume and a factor regime showing SIZE bull z-score +0.14, hinting at sustained small-cap value rotation. INTU +4.19% with 3.4x volume in the Software 7 could be a leader if AI-related software flows accelerate and GEX remains supportive above 625.0 in QQQ. QCOM around +12% with automotive AI catalysts and Semi 7 +3.04% momentum makes it key for gauging whether flows continue to shift from software into semiconductors as BofA and Goldman highlight.
